concerns.sentence: CK elevated [E:note:row472627:3] then negative [E:note:row472627:13], INR elevated [E:note:row472627:9], K+ 3.7 [E:note:row472627:14], and ABG 7.36/56/63 [E:note:row472627:20].
concerns.reason: The term 'negative' is used to describe a CK (Creatine Kinase) result, which is a quantitative enzyme level and not a binary positive/negative test.

recommended_revisions.original: CK elevated [E:note:row472627:3] then negative [E:note:row472627:13], INR elevated [E:note:row472627:9], K+ 3.7 [E:note:row472627:14], and ABG 7.36/56/63 [E:note:row472627:20].
recommended_revisions.suggested: CK elevated [E:note:row472627:3] then neg [E:note:row472627:13], INR elevated [E:note:row472627:9], K+ 3.7 [E:note:row472627:14], and ABG 7.36/56/63 [E:note:row472627:20].
recommended_revisions.reason: Aligns terminology with the fact sheet's 'neg' to avoid clinical misinterpretation of a quantitative lab as a binary result.
